result.summary: ZITADEL exposes tenant-hosted HTTP APIs for resource management (v2), legacy Auth/Management/Admin/System (v1, with System self-hosted only), and SCIM v2 provisioning, all authenticated with Bearer tokens—either OAuth access tokens or service-account PATs for most APIs, and a self-signed JWT bearer token for the self-hosted System API.

oauth_access_token.label: OAuth 2.0 access token for a user or service account
oauth_access_token.setup: For API access, create a service account in the ZITADEL console and authenticate it using one of ZITADEL's supported methods: private key JWT, client credentials, or a personal access token, following [Authenticate service accounts](https://zitadel.com/docs/guides/integrate/service-accounts/authenticate-service-accounts). To call ZITADEL APIs (except Auth/System), request a token that includes the reserved audience scope `urn:zitadel:iam:org:project:id:zitadel:aud` as described in [Access ZITADEL APIs](https://zitadel.com/docs/guides/integrate/zitadel-apis/access-zitadel-apis). For user-scoped Auth API calls, use the authenticated user's access token instead.

system_api_jwt.label: Self-signed JWT for the self-hosted System API user
system_api_jwt.setup: For self-hosted ZITADEL only, configure a System API user in runtime settings and register its public key as described in [Access ZITADEL System API](https://zitadel.com/docs/guides/integrate/zitadel-apis/access-zitadel-system-api). Generate an RSA keypair, add the public key under `SystemAPIUsers`, then mint a self-signed JWT with claims `iss`, `sub`, `aud`, `iat`, and `exp` matching your configured system user and ZITADEL base URL. The docs also show using [ZITADEL Tools](https://github.com/zitadel/zitadel-tools) `key2jwt` to create a test token.

surfaces.name: ZITADEL resource APIs (v2)
surfaces.notes: ZITADEL recommends the v2 resource-based APIs for new integrations. Docs describe them as REST and gRPC APIs, but the public pages scraped did not expose a machine-readable OpenAPI URL.

surfaces.name: ZITADEL Auth API (v1)
surfaces.notes: The Auth API is for operations on the currently authenticated user and uses the authenticated user's token rather than a service account.

surfaces.name: ZITADEL Management API (v1)
surfaces.notes: Legacy v1 API for organization-scoped administrative tasks.

surfaces.name: ZITADEL Admin API (v1)
surfaces.notes: Legacy v1 instance-level administrative API. The Event API functionality is exposed via Admin API endpoints such as `/admin/v1/events/_search`.

surfaces.name: ZITADEL SCIM v2 API
surfaces.notes: Preview SCIM service provider interface for user provisioning. Supports only the SCIM User schema at present.
